questions.questionText:
A regular polygon has interior angles of 144°.
Find the number of sides of the polygon and state its order of rotational symmetry.
workingSteps: A regular decagon has 10 lines of symmetry and rotational order 10.
questions.explanation: For regular polygons, number of sides = 360° ÷ exterior angle.

questions.questionText:
A kite has vertices at A(0, 4), B(3, 0), C(0, −2) and D(−3, 0).
Explain why the y-axis is a line of symmetry of the kite.
questions.correctAnswer: B and D are reflections of each other in the y-axis; A and C lie on the y-axis.

markScheme.desc: B(3, 0) and D(−3, 0) are reflections of each other in y-axis
A(0, 4) and C(0, −2) lie on the y-axis
Therefore the y-axis is a line of symmetry

workingSteps: B(3, 0) reflects to (−3, 0) = D; D reflects to (3, 0) = B
A(0, 4) and C(0, −2) have x-coordinate 0, so they lie on the y-axis
The shape is unchanged by reflection in the y-axis
questions.explanation: A line of symmetry maps the shape onto itself — each vertex maps to a vertex of the shape.

questions.context: Shape S has vertices at (2, 1), (5, 1), (5, 4) and (2, 4).

parts.label: a
parts.questionText: Name the shape S and state its number of lines of symmetry.
workingSteps: All four sides have length 3 cm (e.g., (2,1) to (5,1) = 3).
All angles are 90°. It is a square.
A square has 4 lines of symmetry.

parts.label: b
parts.questionText: Write down the equations of all lines of symmetry of shape S.

markScheme.desc: Two diagonal lines: y = x − 1 and y = −x + 6 oe

workingSteps: Diagonal through (2,1) and (5,4): slope 1, passes through (2,1): y = x − 1
Diagonal through (5,1) and (2,4): slope −1, passes through (5,1): y = −x + 6

parts.label: c
parts.questionText: State the order of rotational symmetry of shape S.
workingSteps: A square maps onto itself after rotations of 90°, 180°, 270° and 360°.
